title: Certificate-Based OPC-UA Logon Failures and Weak Trust Stores

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua" earliest=-7d
| search status!="Good" OR match(_raw,"(?i)BadCertificate|issuer|untrusted")
| eval server=coalesce(opcua_server_endpoint, dest_host)
| stats count by server, status, opcua_security_mode, opcua_user_token_type
| sort - count
description: Tracks OPC-UA secure channel failures and untrusted issuer patterns to validate strength of public-key authentication for industrial servers.
value: Tracks OPC-UA secure channel failures and untrusted issuer patterns to validate strength of public-key authentication for industrial servers.
grandmaExplanation: We track OPC-UA secure channel failures and untrusted issuer patterns to validate strength of public-key authentication for industrial servers — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Deploy UA GDS or centralized PKI; (2) map status codes to runbooks; (3) alert on spike per server; (4) rotate OPC-UA app certs on schedule.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub / unified `index=ot_protocol`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `edge_hub_opcua` or unified sourcetype with `security_policy`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Deploy UA GDS or centralized PKI; (2) map status codes to runbooks; (3) alert on spike per server; (4) rotate OPC-UA app certs on schedule.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua" earliest=-7d
| search status!="Good" OR match(_raw,"(?i)BadCertificate|issuer|untrusted")
| eval server=coalesce(opcua_server_endpoint, dest_host)
| stats count by server, status, opcua_security_mode, opcua_user_token_type
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Certificate-Based OPC-UA Logon Failures and Weak Trust Stores (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.9)** — Tracks OPC-UA secure channel failures and untrusted issuer patterns to validate strength of public-key authentication for industrial servers.

Documented **Data sources**: `edge_hub_opcua` or unified sourcetype with `security_policy`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub / unified `index=ot_protocol`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_opcua. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **server** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by server, status, opcua_security_mode, opcua_user_token_type** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Time chart, Single value (failed channels).
visualization: Table, Time chart, Single value (failed channels).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
